hypothesis: Fine-tuning a pre-trained eSEN model with a small targeted DFT dataset of ≤100 NEB-pathway images that includes explicit stress tensors and employs a loss function weighting stress twice as heavily as energy will reduce the RMSE of predicted stress components by ≥50% and decrease the number of VASP-restart SSNEB iterations to convergence by ≥30% for both CsPbI3 and TiO2 transition pathways.
